page: Microsoft Azure HB120-64rs_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the HBrsv2 family. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 456.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 7.12 GB per core.

This server configuration includes 1452 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. Additionally, the instance is RDMA (Remote Direct Memory Access) capable, which supports low-latency, high-throughput networking interfaces. It does not include any graphics processing units or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated compute resources, positioning it for memory-intensive workloads. The inclusion of local storage and RDMA capability makes it suitable for distributed database systems, large-scale simulations, and high-performance computing tasks requiring node-to-node communication without GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure HB120-64rs_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server within the HBrsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor, paired with 456.0 GB of system memory, which provides 7.12 GB of RAM per core. For storage, the instance includes 1452 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable. This server is also RDMA capable, enabling high-throughput, low-latency networking across nodes. With no integrated GPUs, this configuration is designed for memory-intensive and network-bound workloads. The high memory capacity and dedicated CPU allocation make it suitable for large-scale databases, high-performance computing simulations, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
